title: alchemilla vulgaris var. euobtusa
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris var. euobtusa, also known as lady's mantle, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of lady's mantle have been used in herbal medicine due to their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of lady's mantle are said to help with wound healing, menstrual disorders, and gastrointestinal issues, offering health benefits such as reducing menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding, as well as soothing digestive issues. Lady's mantle can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or infused in oil, making it a versatile herbal preparation for various health needs.
